claims.evidence_text: The initial amount of residue after harvest (lbs/A) is calculated by multiplying the wheat residue coefficient (100 lbs residue/bushel grain) by the long-term yield (ex. 35 bu/A wheat is equivalent to 3,500 lbs/A residue after harvest). Percent cover after harvest can be assumed at 95
claims.claim_summary: Wheat residue after harvest = 100 lbs residue per bushel grain; ~95% percent cover after harvest before tillage operations.

claims.evidence_text: The initial amount of wheat residue in lbs/A or percent cover can be reduced for overwinter weathering, grazing, tillage and planting operations... Moldboard plow [10%]... Chisel plow straight shovel points [75%]... Disk (tandem or offset) 3 inches deep [70%]
claims.claim_summary: Wheat residue cover is reduced by tillage operations: moldboard plow leaves ~10%, chisel plow ~60-80%, tandem disk ~70-90% of residue remaining.
